name: Goodfield State Bank
description_short: Independent community bank serving central Illinois since 1914, offering checking, savings, mortgages, and business banking across six locations.
description_long:
Goodfield State Bank was founded in December 1914 by W.R. Simpson, Byron Colburn, and S.E. Naffziger in a country store, becoming officially incorporated as a state-chartered institution on January 3, 1921, with initial assets of $123,000. The bank has grown to over $100 million in assets as of 2015 and remains locally owned and managed, with over 220 shareholders primarily tied to surrounding communities. The institution operates as a Subchapter S corporation under Goodfield Financial Corporation, its holding company established in 2003.

Goodfield State Bank provides a comprehensive range of retail and business banking services including personal and business checking accounts (with some earning variable interest), mortgages with multiple options, mobile banking, and online account access available 24/7. Their business checking accounts require a $10 monthly service charge with no minimum deposit to open, though a $1,000 minimum balance is needed to earn interest. The bank also offers a Business Academy featuring educational content on financial topics and banking basics.

What distinguishes Goodfield State Bank is its deeply rooted local ownership and long operational history in central Illinois. Unlike larger regional or national banks, the majority of shareholders have direct ties to the communities served, and the bank has systematically expanded from its original Goodfield location to six branches across Goodfield, Eureka, Metamora, Roanoke, Washington, and Varna. The institution maintains a commitment to independent operation rather than consolidation with larger holding companies.

For consumers, Goodfield State Bank functions as a traditional community bank with standard retail and commercial banking products. The main limitation is geographic reach—service is confined to central Illinois locations—and the website provides limited detail on specific product terms, rates, or fees beyond basic checking account information. Customers should contact branches directly for comprehensive pricing, mortgage rates, and loan terms.
services: Personal checking accounts (free and interest-bearing options)
Business checking accounts with variable interest rates
Local branch banking at six locations
pros: Locally owned and independently operated since 1914 with deep community ties
Six branch locations across central Illinois for convenient in-person banking
24/7 online and mobile banking access for checking and account management
Business checking accounts with no minimum deposit requirement to open
Variable interest-bearing business checking options at competitive rates
Comprehensive mortgage options and Business Academy educational resources
Over $100 million in assets provides institutional stability and FDIC coverage
cons: Limited geographic footprint—only serves central Illinois, not suitable for customers outside the region
Website lacks specific rate information, fee schedules, and product terms for comparison shopping
No mention of ATM network partnerships or nationwide fee-free ATM access
Limited detail on mortgage product types (FHA, VA, refinance options) compared to specialized lenders
No online account opening process described; requires in-person or phone application
best_for: Central Illinois residents seeking traditional community banking with local ownership
Small business owners needing relationship-based business checking and lending
Homebuyers in the bank's service area looking for personalized mortgage guidance
Customers prioritizing local economic reinvestment and community bank support
diagnosis: Goodfield State Bank is best for central Illinois residents and small business owners seeking traditional community banking with local ownership and personalized service. The main caveat is geographic limitation—the bank operates only in a six-location footprint across central Illinois and is not accessible to customers outside this region.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
